works.title: Man and Technics
works.subtitle: A Contribution to a Philosophy of Life
works.author: Oswald Spengler
works.translator: JD Jones with Claude (fresh translation from the 1931 German)

units.title: Introduction

summary.one_line: The whole thesis in miniature: history has a form, Cultures are organisms, and the West is in its winter.
summary.student: Spengler announces a science of history. Cultures, like plants, pass through spring, summer, autumn and winter and then harden into 'Civilization'. The familiar 'ancient–medieval–modern' scheme is a parochial Western conceit; in its place he offers a 'Copernican' view in which every Culture is one organism among several, each with its own life-cycle. Our own age, he argues, is the West's late autumn passing into winter.
summary.why_it_matters: Everything in the two volumes is an unfolding of the claims compressed into this chapter. Read it slowly and the rest of the book becomes a vast set of worked examples.

units.title: The Meaning of Numbers

summary.one_line: Every Culture has its own mathematic; number is a symbol of the soul, not a universal truth.

units.title: The Problem of World-history (1): Physiognomic and Systematic

summary.one_line: Two ways of knowing: the systematic (Nature, causality) and the physiognomic (History, destiny).

units.title: The Problem of World-history (2): The Destiny-idea and the Causality-principle

summary.one_line: Destiny is the logic of time; Causality the logic of space. History can only be felt, not calculated.

units.title: Makrokosmos (1): The Symbolism of the World-picture and the Problem of Space

summary.one_line: A Culture's whole world grows from one root-intuition of space — its prime-symbol.

units.title: Makrokosmos (2): Apollinian, Faustian, and Magian Soul

summary.one_line: The three souls named: Apollinian (the body), Magian (the cavern), Faustian (infinite space).

units.title: Music and Plastic (1): The Arts of Form

summary.one_line: Each Culture chooses its arts: the statue for the Classical, the fugue and oil-painting for the West.

units.title: Music and Plastic (2): Act and Portrait

summary.one_line: The nude and the portrait: Classical surface against Faustian depth and biography.

units.title: Soul-image and Life-feeling (1): On the Form of the Soul

summary.one_line: Psychology is itself a Culture-form: each soul pictures itself in its own image.

units.title: Soul-image and Life-feeling (2): Buddhism, Stoicism, and Socialism

summary.one_line: Buddhism, Stoicism and Socialism: the 'contemporary' final moralities of three Cultures.

units.title: Faustian and Apollinian Nature-knowledge

summary.one_line: Even physics is a faith: each Culture's science is a myth of its own soul, and ours dissolves into pure function.

units.title: Origin and Landscape (A): The Cosmic and the Microcosm

summary.one_line: Life in two keys: the cosmic 'being' that pulses in the blood, and the waking-being of the free-moving microcosm.

units.title: Origin and Landscape (B): The Group of the Higher Cultures

summary.one_line: Why the high Cultures are so few, how they stand together as a group, and what 'world-history' really surveys.

units.title: Origin and Landscape (C): The Relations between the Cultures

summary.one_line: How Cultures meet, borrow and deform one another — the germ of the 'pseudomorphosis' worked out later.

units.title: Cities and Peoples (A): The Soul of the City

summary.one_line: World-history is the history of the city: the city turns peasant into citizen, and at last into the nomad of the metropolis.

units.title: Cities and Peoples (B): Peoples, Races, Tongues

summary.one_line: Race, people and tongue are not biology but destiny — what a landscape and a history stamp on a stream of being.

units.title: Cities and Peoples (C): Primitives, Culture-Peoples, Fellaheen

summary.one_line: Before and after a Culture: the primitive peoples that come before it, and the 'fellaheen' who outlive it.

units.title: Problems of the Arabian Culture (A): Historic Pseudomorphoses

summary.one_line: When a young soul is forced into the alien mould of an older Culture — the Magian cramped beneath Classical forms.

units.title: Problems of the Arabian Culture (B): The Magian Soul

summary.one_line: The Magian world: the cavern-feeling, the divine substance, the consensus of the faithful — the soul behind the great religions.

units.title: Problems of the Arabian Culture (C): Pythagoras, Mohammed, Cromwell

summary.one_line: Religion in the springtime of three Cultures — Classical cult, Magian revelation, Faustian Puritanism.

units.title: The State (A): The Problem of the Estates — Nobility and Priesthood

summary.one_line: The great estates — nobility and priesthood, time against space, the manor against the cathedral — as a Culture's living form.

units.title: The State (B): State and History

summary.one_line: The state as a people held 'in form': the history-making minority, the political stream, and the meaning of the great event.

units.title: The State (C): Philosophy of Politics

summary.one_line: What it means to be 'in form' politically — the statesman against the masses, and the state's slow sinking into mere economics.

units.title: The Form-world of Economic Life (A): Money

summary.one_line: Money as a Faustian way of thinking — abstract, dynamic — that turns the world into book-keeping and dissolves the old estates.

units.title: The Form-world of Economic Life (B): The Machine

summary.one_line: The machine as the Faustian will-to-power made iron: the engineer, the entrepreneur, and the gathering revolt against the machine.

units.title: Technics as the Tactics of Living

summary.one_line: Technics is not the tool but the tactics of living — the whole way a being wages its war with the world.

units.title: Herbivores and Beasts of Prey

summary.one_line: Two forms of life: the herbivore that flees and the beast of prey that hunts — and man, by his soul, is a beast of prey.

units.title: The Origin of Man: Hand and Tool

summary.one_line: The hand and the tool make man: the free hand that seizes a weapon turns the beast of prey into the maker.

units.title: The Second Stage: Speech and Enterprise

summary.one_line: Speech and the deed-in-common: work is organized, the 'enterprise' is born, and the leaders divide from the led.

units.title: The Last Act: Rise and End of the Machine Culture

summary.one_line: The Faustian machine-culture masters the world — and races toward its own exhaustion and end.
